entry.title: Re: Story Report Field Navigation Help

entry.title: Re: Tracing call-off purchase orders negotiated savings
entry.content:
Hi,

You can use the condition supplements to add required discount conditions in the contract, so that it will be copied to call off automatically.

In ME32K, select the item and click on conditions button. Then maintain the required discount conditions. The same shall be done at header level as well.

Regards,

If you think there could be improvement in my work, please let me know. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)

Hi @David_Chaviano 

I am trying to claim campaign code but I am getting below error, pls guide.

entry.title: Some questions about S4Core 108
entry.content:
While operating in S4Core 108, I have met some questions.
 
1.I want to transport migration project from DEV 100 to DEV 110(different clients in one system). I tried two methods but both failed.
 
   1.1 I use the method "goto - use project in different client" in LTMOM,but failed.error message is  "Project is already assigned to the current client".(already choose the development package newly created instead of $TMP)
 
   1.2 I use LTMOM to add the migration project to the TR in DEV 100,but when l try to use SCC1N to transport it to DEV110, failed. the message said "Before Export Phase Failed. Please check Log for Details"(I am sure that the migration project is fully included in the TR, and the TR is newly created and don't include other conception. Already use SM12 to check and found no table locked in username.Already use SU53 to check and found no authorization lack.)
 
 
2.Another question is about "import financial plan data". I use it to upload project cost plan instead of CJ40. but fiscal year is mandatory,then how can i upload the overall cost of project?
 
3.And i found that the fiscal year data needs to be maintained in OKEQ CO version 0,but my upload data's plan version is P01. So why is the fiscal year data controlled by version 0's configuration instead of version P01's configuration?
 
4.What' more, I also found that WBS must be marked as Statistical, then the fiacal year can be controlled by config OKEQ. If WBS not be marked as Statistical, the fiacal year can be any year and out of control.  Why does this need to be associated with WBS attributes "Statistical"?

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Strategy - End-to-end business processes deep dives - NEW eBook available
entry.content: Thank you for share such nice document!

entry.title: Re: Service and Asset Manager - notficiation type not appearing
entry.content:
Where exactly are they not appearing? Are you creating them on the client and they are not appearing on the list or are you creating them on the backend and expecting them in the client?
Please also mention the persona you are using along with the metadata version.
For the FST persona, the types are filtered using UserObjectType. Check the ServiceNotificationTypesQueryOption.js rule.

entry.title: Re: How can I clear the WIP balance in TECO production or...
entry.content: "The 'Manage Event-Based Posting Errors - Product Costing' app is just a view-only screen. Should I use the app below instead? 'Postprocess Event-Based Postings - Product Costing'"

entry.title: Re: How can I find a freight Order that has multiple Sale...
entry.content:
One fool-proof way would be to to write an SQL query via SQ00, where you can check the TOR_ITEM table, with item category = PRD, and the base business document contains the sales order number. You can then in ALV grid display just do a unique count of the column. 

Other way, not foolproof, would be to use the TORROT label field. usually this field will contain the sales order/delivery number if there is only unique sales order/delivery/trq.

entry.title: Re: Task 1 - Create CDS View Entity ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content: My solution.

entry.title: Re: SAP RE-FX: % Share in Source Condition when the sourc...
entry.content:
Hi,

The calculation formula 'E2 % Share in Source Condition' uses the source condition's amount for its calculation.

In your case, the sales-based rent condition is the source, which uses the calculation formula "U." This will not work because the formula "U" does not calculate the condition amount instead, it serves as a technical parameter for the sales-based settlement.

Please use a source condition that calculates the condition amount.

Best regards,

Achyuth reddy.

entry.title: Re: Activate "Self Registration form" SAP IAS
entry.content: Hi Istvan, I understand your reply on setting up trust between Idp and our application.Is this the same process whether it is SAML 2.0 or Open ID connect application?

entry.title: Re: RE-FX - Contract Valuation - RECECN - Upload Mass
entry.content:
Hi Berubean,

You can use BUS1505_CHANGE to update/Create valuation parameter in the contract.

Best Regards,

Achyuth.

entry.title: Caused by: EvalError: Refused to evaluate a string as JavaScript because 'unsafe-eval' is not an all
entry.content: hi, I'm used QR code library in SAP my UI5 application.. it's worked for locally & deploy but in SAP Work Zone  i created tile for this application...it is not working ....getting error  like this "Caused by: EvalError: Refused to evaluate a string as JavaScript because 'unsafe-eval' is not an allowed source of script in the following Content Security Policy directive: "script-src * data: blob:".  plz anyone help help me.

entry.title: No projects available for my Desktop Agent
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I'm trying to re-use an automation scenario from store(SAP Build Process Automation).I have followed the steps https://help.sap.com/docs/IRPA_S4HANA/144f48c43d774333b158cf83044967fa/567290119cdb41d691d530c0639d2201.html, but at step 5, I do not see any projects in my agent, neither 'start' / 'record' button being visible at the agent. The agent is connected and visible in SAP Build Process Automation -> Environment -> Agents.. Projects are visible at Environment -> Projects.

Not sure,

whether 'service state' is anything to do with this problem (or)

mismatch of version at agent vs project is the problem (or)

anything to do with trigger condition ???

Any help will be appreciated!! Thanks in advance!!

SAP Build Process Automation @Archana @vipin_Ojha

Jitin

entry.title: Re: Step Into Autonomy: Introduction to Configuration Manager
entry.content:
Hi ,

Informative blog . 

Regards ,

entry.title: Re: How to pass header and line item xml body to the CPI...
entry.content:
Hi @Akash 

In CPI (Cloud Platform Integration), you can manage multiple headers and line items based on your specific requirements. Here are two examples to illustrate how you might handle these:

1. Data Transformation: If you need to transform or manipulate data from headers and line items, CPI provides options to use message mapping. This allows you to customize how data is processed and formatted.

2. Custom Logging: If your goal is to include header and line-item information in a custom header for logging purposes in message monitoring, you can achieve this using Groovy or JavaScript scripts. These scripting options let you tailor the logging to fit your needs.

Could you please clarify your specific requirements so we can assist you more effectively?

Regards,

AK

SAP Integration Suite | Fiori | ABAP Architect

entry.title: Re: Subcontracting indicator in MD04 S/4HANA
entry.content: Remove tag SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sourcing and Procurement.

entry.title: Re: UPDATE statement delete rows
entry.content:
You need the full row as it was before in your Work Area (the variabel structure you are updating from). Like this where data is read into Work Area before updating:

DATA wa TYPE scustom.
SELECT SINGLE *
       FROM scustom
       WHERE id = '00017777'
       INTO @wa.
wa-discount = '003'.
UPDATE scustom FROM @wa.

Otherwise you need to update specific field with this:

UPDATE <table> set <FIELDNAME> = 'xxx'
where key1 eq xxx
and    key2 eq yyy...

entry.title: Re: Is it impossible to connect by client when connecting...
entry.content:
No, there are no client dependent RFC server registrations - not even SAP system dependent ones.
RFC request dispatching for registered servers is only done via gateway host and program ID parameters.

entry.title: Moving Average Price control
entry.content:
Hello,

We are currently migrating to SAP, part of the migrating is taking over the inventory values for materials. Most of the material types will have a price control "Moving Average". However, for a considerable amount of materials the current price is missing. Besides manual enrichment, has anyone ever encountered this situation and had a more time efficient approach to this problem? 

Thanks!

#materialmaster

entry.title: Re: A standard function module is overwriting my logic in...
entry.content:
You can use a Business Transaction Event in the end of the posting. 

Tcode FIBF

It´s a bit tricky until you get the drill, but basically you wil lhave to find the right BTE, create a Function Module and code it. Create a Product (and activate) and assign the FM to the Product.

Best regards, William

Good day

I have been working as an administrative coordinator for a mechanical construction company since 2019, and I did my BE in 2006. Now I want to switch to an IT job at the age of 40. Please advise on studying SAP and which module will have more job scope in the future.

I am eagerly looking forward to your advice, team.

Thanks

Bharathidasan Murugesan

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to realize the line data access contro...
entry.content: Yes, you can achieve that in SAC via Data access control, check this help page for detail: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/b46f2f74aea04c399ccb5c9b1bcc225e.html?q=data%20access%20control

entry.title: Re: Service and Asset Manager - notficiation type not appearing
entry.content:
Hello,

where did you set the Notification Types in Syclo?

There are two oMDOs where you can set types for Notifications:

  • SAMXXXX_NOTIFICATION_GENERIC: Here you can set which Notifs you want to read/show on the SSAM from the backend - defined with an assignment type
  • SAMXXXX_NOTIF_TYPE: Here you can set which Notification types should be visibile on Notification create page

Regards

Björn

entry.title: will incomplete TECO of a production order impact MRP ??
entry.content: I have a situation where i have list of production orders where TECO is not completed when MRP runs its picks up the demand and shows in correct call-off - can some one help to understand this issue. in my view if the Teco is not completed the demand should be reflecting for all the parts in that production order

entry.title: SAP webi formula to calculate the hours and minutes between two dates
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Is there a more accurate formula for calculating the difference between two dates in hours and minutes in SAP web intelligence?

I need to determine the time difference between two dates. For instance, given the following dates:

- date1: 07/06/2024 09:52:17 PM
- date2: 07/07/2024 02:25:39 AM

The correct difference is 4:33, but the formula I used yielded a result of 5:01. Can you suggest a better formula to get the correct result?

 

=((((ToNumber(Substr(FormatDate([Performed Date & Time] ; "HH:mm:ss") ; 1 ;2 ) ) * 60 *60+ToNumber(Substr(FormatDate([Order Date & Time] ; "HH:mm:ss") ; 4 ;2) ) * 60 +ToNumber(Substr(FormatDate([Performed Date & Time]; "HH:mm:ss") ; 7 ;2 ) )) - (ToNumber(Substr(FormatDate([Order Date & Time] ; "HH:mm:ss") ; 1 ;2 ) ) * 60 *60+ToNumber(Substr(FormatDate([Order Date & Time] ; "HH:mm:ss") ; 4 ;2) ) * 60 +ToNumber(Substr(FormatDate([Order Date & Time] ; "HH:mm:ss") ; 7 ;2 ) ) ))) / 60 + (DaysBetween([Order Date & Time]; [Performed Date & Time]) * 24 * 60) ) / 60

 

Regards.

entry.title: RABAX error on sap gui logon
entry.content:
Hi,

How to fix error during sap gui logon.
No updates been made on server, and upon checking of R3trans -d, no error also.
Upon checking on other system via SM59 then remote sap logon to system having "Rabax during sap gui logon"
on Transaction: ST22 there's a lot of errors
- Runtime Error: DBSQL_SQL_ERROR
- Exception: CX_SY_OPEN_SQL_DB

via server command line, we already executed the R3Trans - d command with no error.
for the db space, there's still a lot of space.

What should we do next to fix the error?


Thank You

entry.title: How to create a list of SAP HANA parameters
entry.content:
I am creating a list of SAP HANA parameters.
It seemed like a good idea to use the SYS.M_INIFILE_CONTENTS view to reference all the currently set HANA parameters at once.

This SYS.M_INIFILE_CONTENTS view exists in both systemDB and TenantDB, and each contains different information.

For example, if you search for LAYER_NAME = 'SYSTEM' in the SYS.M_INIFILE_CONTENTS view in TenantDB, several parameters will be found, and if you similarly search the SYS.M_INIFILE_CONTENTS view in SystemDB, several different parameters will be found.

Also, if you search the SYS.M_INIFILE_CONTENTS view by narrowing down the KEY to one, data for the three LAYER_NAMEs "DEFAULT", "SYSTEM", and "DATABASE" will be returned as results.

When creating a parameter list for SAP HANA, how should I organize the SYS.M_INIFILE_CONTENTS views in systemDB and TenantDB?

entry.title: Re: PO Collaboration with Central Procurement and the Ari...
entry.content: As your looking to integrate S/4 HANA Central Procurement Hub solution with Ariba Network by sending the Purchase Order Output via EDI channel. This new integration scenario or new scope item (6BJ) is currently under development, and we are planning to release it in the upcoming release tentatively*. To know the right release timelines, we would suggest you check the SAP Roadmap explorer.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere OData service
entry.content: Please refer the document below: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/43509d67b8b84e66a30851e832f66911/7a453609c8694b029493e7d87e0de60a.html#parameters

entry.title: Causes leading to widespread property changes in SAP Fiori screens
entry.content:
Context - 

We have observed lot of property changes in SAP Fiori screen in S4 regression system which has caused failure to Automated regression suite over the weekend while executing for MTP.

These changes have necessitated substantial modifications (70-80%) to the automation scripts to prevent further failures. 

These property changes include:

Old

property for field to enter Tcode = Enter Transaction code (upper case T)

New

property for field to enter Tcode = Enter transaction code (lower case t)

Close button on SAP Fiori Screen got unmapped from scripts because it had property as Link earlier and now it is changed to Button.

Similar way there were many properties which got changed and would be difficult to summarize here.

 

Questions -

What could have caused these widespread property changes in SAP Fiori screens? Are there any recent updates, upgrades, or configuration changes that might have triggered this, and the reason how these upgrades led to these property changes?

Additionally, what are the recommended best practices for handling such UI changes in automation scripts to minimize their impact on regression testing?

entry.title: Re: Sales in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2005
entry.content:
Dear Experts

I want to know in Import Sales Order template, is it possible to make an optional field mandatory, Please confirm & if possible then how to do the same

Thanks

Rahul Singhal

entry.title: No of Same Work Center
entry.content:
Hi all,

My client's requirement is like this

the client has 7 lathe machines on the shop floor sometimes he uses one lathe machine for one order, sometimes he uses two lathe machines, sometimes he uses all lathe machines for one order. sometimes he uses 1,2,3 for one order and 4,5,6,7 for another order, the client does not want to have  7 different work centers due to more no of production versions and routing.

He wants to group them all into one and use them based on req.

Is there any possible solution for this if there please help.

Thanks 

Raviram

entry.title: Re: Sales in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition – Automated Order Process with SAP´s Intelligent Tech
entry.content:
In Import Sales Order App, is it possible to change the field property from optional to mandatory, if yeh then how

Thanks 

Rahul Singhal

entry.title: Re: Certification for Partner AI Apps on SAP BTP – Ensuring Reliability, Responsibility, and Relevan
entry.content:
Hi Cocquerel, 

Referring to these documentations (listed below) could be good alternatives of 'AI Dev Guide' in my opinion. these are publicly available documentations for customer as well.

AI For Application Development | AI Foundation in SAP BTP

SAP AI Core | SAP Help Portal

Artificial Intelligence | SAP Help Portal

Responsible AI --> AI Ethics policy guide and handbook

What is the SAP BTP Guidance Framework? | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Where are Tcode that are deprecated in S/4 and a new one that added to S/4 HANA?
entry.content: Is it only transaction codes, or any minor/major change in existing transaction codes? Why transaction codes only? The only way I know is just to keep you informed via "what's new" blog posts, documentation, etc.

entry.title: Is there any filter for Q&A community by SAP products?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Now the new community has been launched, but the questions in Q&A can't be easily identified by different SAP products, for example: S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, etc., so it brings efforts to determine which product the questions are coming from, since I am only focusing on S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, and other colleagues may focus on ECC, S/4HANA OP.

So it is better to add a mandatory field when posting a question to identity the specific SAP products. it can be identity which product belongs to from the default page. see screenshot below

 

Br, Bob

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 Flexibility: Adapting UIs for Specific User Roles
entry.content: @RaminS : Yes, by setting authorization on cards, you can ensure that a user only sees cards from an application for which they have the proper authorization. Please follow this documentation for more details.

entry.title: SAP Unable to Preview and Save as PDF
entry.content:
Hi Expert

need help to resolved this issue on user account

note:  we created user on Windows Server and user is connecting via RDP on server.

          Other user account has no issue on pdf preview only this particular user

when we try to preview the PDF on SAP Client and it show this the below error 

"Could not find a part of the path 'C:\Users\user5\AppData\Local\Temp\24\B1CRPROXY_CONNSTR_0000E334_0.tmp'."
 
Thank you for your help
Zernan

entry.title: Unauthorized Error When Accessing SAP Product API from Browser
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm able to access the product API in the SAP client gateway, but when I try to access it from the browser, it prompts for a username and password. After entering my SAP username and password, I receive an "unauthorized" error. Can anyone help with this issue? Any suggestions would be helpful.

entry.title: Re: Return the System Status from ORAP (Order Approved) t...
entry.content:
Hello,

Can I know the business need to revert status to ORAI from ORAP?

Phase control codes can be configured in such a way that it will be set on an entity when the entity reaches a phase or subphase or used to block the entity attaining a particular phase or subphase.

For more information, please refer to below help documents.

Phase Control Codes | SAP Help Portal

Configuring Phase Control Codes | SAP Help Portal

Please refer below blog for configuration of phase control code with example.

Phase control codes for Maintenance Orders in SAP ... - SAP Community

Hope this helps.

BR,

Vijayalaxmi Melavanki

entry.title: Re: RABAX error on sap gui logon
entry.content: Can't login at all? How can you start transaction ST22 if you can't login?

entry.title: Re: What is the equivalent app for FCHN in SAP S/4HANA Cl...
entry.content:
Please refer to KBA 3037953 - Manage Outgoing Checks (App ID F1578) for more details.

Below are the relevant information regarding the app Manage Outgoing Checks:

  • Manage Outgoing Checks - SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library
  • Manage Outgoing Checks - SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Delivery Line Item Deletion Validation in VL02N
entry.content: It is not a duplicate line. It is a batch split item. All is good.

entry.title: Re: Horizon Theme and Templates for Stories in SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: Re: SAP ARIBA integration with a third-party system
entry.content:
Hey @Tural-Hajiyev,

SAP integration suite would be a good starting point for your research. Have a look here: https://api.sap.com/package/SAPBusinessNetworkIntegrationwithNonSAPERP/integrationflow

And this older community thread: https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-q-a/sap-ariba-integration-with-microsoft-dynamics/qaq-p/12151543

Let the community know what you decided in the end with which public source 🙂

KR Martin

entry.title: Re: Is there any filter for Q&A community by SAP products?
entry.content:
All content when posted has  or should have a tag applied "SAP Managed Tags", from there you can use the managed tag pages (i.e. https://community.sap.com/t5/c-khhcw49343/SAP+S%25252F4HANA+Cloud+Public+Edition/pd-p/08e2a51b-1ce5-4367-8b33-4ae7e8b702e0) to then see specifically those items with that tag.

In the next few days a new release will be deployed and you will be able to filter by content type on those managed tag pages.

entry.content: What is the real problem here? You said: "MRP runs its picks up the demand and shows in correct call-off". So is it correct or not? Please provide more details.

entry.title: Re: TM Freight Unit Source dates setup for consolidation
entry.content: Hello dear do you know of any BADI that we can indicate that assigns the same date to everything?

entry.title: Outlook interview scheduling - M365 Mail.ReadWrite & Mail.Send permission
entry.content:
Hi, we are currently implementing the Outlook interview scheduling integration from SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting. This requires our Azure/M365 to give the following permissions to the application

- Mail.ReadWrite

- Mail.Send 

We can find the following information from SuccessFactors on why these permissions are needed:

Mail.ReadWrite
To create an upload session to attach documents to calendar events if the size of the document is greater than 3 MB
POST https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users/{id | userPrincipalName}/events/{id}/attachments/createUploadSession
Attachments of the application and email templates
  • Meeting ID
  • User principle name
Mail.Send
To forward an event message using JSON format or MIME content
POST https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users/{id | userPrincipalName}/messages/{id}/forward
Content of the event message
User principle name

However, our security team wants to know if this permission is only applicable for our service account user, or for all users in our organization. If the second, how can we limit this?

entry.title: Re: Revaluated as 'original account assignment'
entry.content: Thank you for your answer.

entry.title: Re: Task 1 - Create CDS View Entity ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content: Task 1...Done...!

entry.title: Re: Revaluated as 'original account assignment'
entry.content: After setting up CBC, when I created a new PO with a price difference, it was evaluated as a consumable under the original account.

entry.title: Re: Certification for Partner AI Apps on SAP BTP – Ensuring Reliability, Responsibility, and Relevan
entry.content: @ParvathyS_SAP , Thank you, I finally got the 'AI Dev Guide' from a partner. Looking at content, I don't understand why it is not made available to customers.

entry.title: Re: RABAX error on sap gui logon
entry.content:
Hello,

Kindly refer to the SAP notes 2429578 and 3323101 for the runtime error 

Regards

SS

entry.title: Generate Script is not working in SQL SAP Business One
entry.content:
Dear all,

When I am trying to backup via Generate script option then it is showing error as per below attachment. No error comes into entire script but at last it is showing message like Save to Clipboard - error.

Regards,

Nikunj Mehta

entry.title: Re: SAP TM - Auto populate Email Receiver for PPF Action / PPF Output/ PDF Forms
entry.content:
Hi,

Can someone inform me on where we can maintain the "Sender" email address? Currently, it seems like the email address of the user who triggered the "Send to Carrier" action is being used. We have the need to change this email address to the one plant wishes?

Thanks.

Kaya

entry.title: Re: No of Same Work Center
entry.content: Are you doing capacity requirement planning ?

entry.title: in sap how do we calculate the 3 currency at single invoice level
entry.content:
example : company currency : INR

transaction currency : USD

group currency :EUR

entry.title: 回复： Assign tax indicators to tax condition types in SAP S...

entry.title: Integrating low code solutions with Microsoft using SAP Integration Suite has never been easier!
entry.content:
This blog is co-authored with Vinayak Adkoli (Lead Product Manager, SAP Integration Suite, SAP SE)

Dear community,

Extending SAP with low-code platforms significantly increases the speed of development, enabling rapid innovation essential for staying competitive today.

Analysts predict that low code will become the preferred software development method by 2025. (KPMG, 2023)

Forrester approximates the citizen development market to be valued at 30 billion dollars by 2028. (Forrester, 2024)

However, it is crucial to maintain stringent security measures and respect existing SAP authorizations. By doing so, organizations can harness the benefits of low-code development while ensuring the protection and compliance of their SAP environment.

Oh boy, you ready for all the solutions, apps, curious interns, and mad scientists looking to interact with SAP ERP to combine with Microsoft 365? 😮

Fear no more! The API Management capability of SAP Integration Suite is more than ready. In our usual Microsoft + SAP co-engineering fashion, we are proud to jointly release a fully-fledged enterprise-grade API management policy to support the integration pattern.

Architecture overview of low code app using SAP APIM for Principal Propagation

It enables SAP Principal Propagation with SAP services such as SAP Gateway, S/4HANA Cloud, RISE, and many more using Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) as Identity Provider. At the core of the solution is the proven OAuth2SAMLBearer flow.

This way users of your low code solutions and apps spanning the Microsoft and SAP ecosystem are mapped from their Microsoft Entra Id identities to their named SAP backend users. SAP authorizations are fully retained!

In addition to that, solving this challenge on Integration Suite level enables scaling the approach to arbitrary many different consumer solutions. No more re-inventing the wheel for every developer!

Find the APIM policy and further guidance here on the SAP API business hub (pending update). Alternative source link🔗

Of course, you may deviate from the blueprint outlined based on your scenario across SAP BTP, SAP Graph, Integration Suite, other SAP SaaS solutions etc.

 

Approach

Principal Propagation Scenarios

OAuth2SAMLBearer flow

Service to service, on-behalf-of user

Authorization Code flow

Interactive user session (prone to MFA interference)

Client Credentials flow

Service to service

X.509

Any

We recommend using OAuth2SAMLBearer, because:

  • The given scenario in this blog is about app integration and identities known to Microsoft Entra ID, for the integration
  • OAuth2 is more flexible and granular control over access to resources
  • NetWeaver does not support Client Credentials flow and X509 certificates come with management overhead.

For simplicity and readability of the blog I will refer only to NetWeaver specific settings even though the approach works with any SAP product that supports OAuth2SAMLBearer.

Learn more about this space overall from the blog series by my magnificent colleague Martin Raepple.

 

A glimpse under the hood

The API Management policy works under the assumption that trust between your OAuth 2.0 Server for AS ABAP and Microsoft Entra ID has been setup before.

Have a look at the developer series on our YouTube playlist for a walk-through experience (be warned this was a “without-script exercise” to show pitfalls and how to overcome).

In addition to the existing authorizations maintained on SAP each application consuming the SAP API proxy from API Management needs to be authorized on Entra ID.

See this official guide for details on the Entra ID SAML2 setup. See the difference for OAuth2SAMLBearer compared to general SAML2 below:

Keep close attention to the Entity ID. It is case sensitive! I chased an error once for half a day because of that.

Be aware that Entity ID must be unique in your Entra ID tenant. In case you want to use SAML2 for Fiori SSO and OAuth2 for SAP Principal Propagation for this SID at the same time, you need to maintain both on the Entra ID enterprise app registration. Assign an order (index) that works with your login flow. See below sample for reference.

Have a look at my video series for a more guided experience on the OAuth2 part. I also like this simple blog series to complete the picture.

Take care of your OAuth settings

The steps for the OAuth configuration may vary a bit by SAP product. Here the focus is on NetWeaver.

Move on to your SAP backend and create a user for your OAuth client. For SAP NetWeaver based systems that will be a user of type system with authorizations for S_SCOPE that are relevant for the OData service you want to expose.

Both the OAuth2 client user and your SAP end user need S_SCOPE authorization.

Use transaction PFCG to assign the authorization objects to your role or create a new one. I like this blog series for reference.

Verify from transaction /n/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE that your OData service is enabled for OAuth2.

Birds eye🐦 view on the overall process

Below sequence diagram explains an initial login performing SAP Principal Propagation using the OAuth2SAMLBearer flow. There are three requests involved:

1. Low Code app login (Entra ID) invoked by the app

2. Token exchange for a SAML2 assertion (Entra ID on-behalf-of flow) invoked by API Management

3. Token exchange of SAML2 assertion issued by Entra ID to SAP access token issued by SAP OAuth2 server. The request is invoked by API Management. The result is a token carrying the authorizations set on the SAP backend (PFCG transaction) for that end user.

As stated at the beginning, the heavy lifting is done by the provided API Management policy.

Once a bearer access token from SAP is available, all requests can be directly served from the API Management token cache. Once it expires – typically after one hour – the refresh token is used to request a new access token. The same is true for the first login step from the low code app.

 

Import the policy into your tenant

Download the policy📥 from the SAP API Business Hub and import the template into your SAP API Management tenant. While policy publish is pending retrieve from alternate source here🔗.

Learn more about configuring an API Provider with SAP Cloud Connector on this SAP tutorial.

Apply to all the PostFlow steps of the TargetEndpoints of your APIs as you see fit. See this SAP tutorial and this SAP GitHub repos for more details. The policy requires to run in the PostFlow section in order for the “target.basepath” to be populated.

Consider the governance and security trade-offs between mighty policies and SAP OAuth clients authorized for everything vs. a multitude of clients for each API and scope.

 

Configure the policy using a key value map

All the configuration needed for earlier shown token exchange flow is best provided with an encoded key value map (I recommend starting with un-encoded one, when you do this the first time for simpler troubleshooting). Create a new encrypted one called “SAPPrincipalPropagationMap”. The name is referenced on the provided policy.

 

Fill the values as per your environment:

 

Key

Value sample

Hints

entra-id-tenant-id

12a345bc-1234-56ab-78ab-zzzzzzzzz

Find it on the Azure portal (e.g. here)

issuer

https://sts.windows.net/12a345bc-1234-56ab-78ab-zzzzzzzzz/

The prefix is fixed. Only the tenant id is dynamic.

 

https://login.microsoftonline.com/12a345bc-1234-56ab-78ab-zzzzzzzzz for v2 endpoints

entra-id-audience

api://999abce-7777-abcd-a6c9-zzzzzzzzzzz

The globally unique Application ID URI from the Entra ID app registration representing your SAP API Management instance

AADRegisteredAppClientId

999abce-7777-abcd-a6c9-zzzzzzzzzzz

The Application (client) id of the Entra ID app registration representing your SAP API Management instance.

AADRegisteredAppClientSecret

 

The secret created for the application 999abce-7777-abcd-a6c9-zzzzzzzzzzz

AADSAPResource

https://a4h100

The provider’s name from your NetWeaver SAML setup. Typically, a URL with SID followed Client number

sap-oauth-client-username

ODATAOAUTH

User name provided on SOAUTH2 transaction (/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/oauth2_config?sap-client=100)

 

Create a system user on SU01 with minimum rights (S_SCOPE) and reference that on SOAUTH2.

Don’t forget to assign authorized scope.

sap-oauth-client-password

 

This is only used to request tokens not to authenticate to SAP.

sap-oauth-scope

ZPRODUCTSVIEW_CDS_0001

The scope assigned on SOAUTH2

SAPOAuthServerAdressForTokenEndpoint

a4h-internal.cloudapp.net:44301

Host and port of the target SAP OAuth server. When cloud connector is used, put the virtual hostname and port.

 

Adjust the name of the API provider as per your setup in the policy elements “RefreshSAPToken”, “fetchSAPOAuthToken”, and “GetCSRFToken”.

 

SAP Cloud Connector settings are minimal

In this scenario all authentication means are done by the SAP API Management policy. Therefore, the configuration for the connected on-premises API provider (your SAP Cloud Connector) is reduced to the host and port only.

Keep Authentication on NONE. But be assured that the OAuth2SAMLBearer flow has your back. Additional auth config on the Cloud Connector would either be redundant or interfere with the setup.

 

Note: The Principal Propagation option on the Cloud Connector connection config uses short-lived X.509 certificates and the purpose of the scenario outlined in this blog was about API only approaches.

 

Authorize the consuming application with API Management

Authorize the Power Automate SAP OData connector to request tokens for your API Management instance using its client id: 6bee4d13-fd19-43de-b82c-4b6401d174c3 assigning the user_impersonation scope.

Verify the id from the Microsoft docs.

 

Next, verify the client id of your API Management instance is authorized on the app registration attached to your target SAP product (in my sample SAP NetWeaver). And because I was lazy, I gave it the same name. Check the required scope is ticked too ("Scopes = 1" on the bottom table of the screenshot below).

 

Be aware that your internal policies might require you to actively assign users or groups to the enterprise app registration. Otherwise, you will get an error before you even get to SAP. Been there, done that. Just saying 😉

 

The final mile of integration

Ok, all homework is done. Now we get to go outside and enjoy the “low code” sun 🌞 Create your SAP OData connection, choose the authentication type Microsoft Entra ID and paste the URI of the Entra ID app registration that represents your API Management.

 

Clicking on Sign in triggers the $metadata request to your OData endpoint to pull available values.

The connection is now authorized with the user you supplied. However, each user with whom the flow is shared will be authorizing its use of the connection on first call again with their identity.

Hints on troubleshooting

  • SAP’s OAuth server has a tracing tool provided as WebDynpro.
    • Open it from SAPGUI with transaction sec_diag_tool or navigate to the web app: /sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/sec_diag_tool?sap-client=YYY
    • Search for error messages and successful mapping of the Entra ID provided email to the SAP backend user.

    •  

  • All the settings are client dependent! Always double check it is being applied (or add sap-client URL parameter to be sure). Been there done that 😉 See below transactions to verify setup:
    • SAML2 or the webdynpro: /sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/saml2?sap-client=YYY
    • SOAUTH2 or the webdynpro: /sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/oauth2_config?sap-client=YYY

 

  • Before applying the APIM policy consider running the sequence of authentication calls locally (with “line of sight” to NetWeaver of course) using a REST client. See this Postman collection for reference. Verify errors from transaction /n/IWFND/ERROR_LOG. Drop cookies in your REST client before re-testing!

 

  • Verify the produced Entra ID tokens attributes using a safe JWT validator (e.g. DevToys). Don’t share your sensitive tokens on some website for validation!
    • iss (Issuer): Should read something like https://sts.windows.net/12a3456-zzz... or https://login.microsoftonline.com/12a3456-zzz... for v2 endpoints.
    • aud (Audience): Something like “api://bbbbbb-cccc-dddd-dddd-eeeeeeee”
    • scp (Scope): “user_impersonation”

 

  • For the SAML2 assertion exercise the same approach but do base64 decode and XML pretty print. Notepad++ with MIME tools -> Base64 decode and XML Tools -> pretty print does the job locally just fine. Again, don’t paste sensitive info online! Verify the following claims from your assertion:
    • AudienceRestriction -> Audience: Should be a URL containing your SID and client id, e.g. https://A4H100
    • Claims: Name, email or whatever you have configured to be used to identify the named SAP backend user.

 

  • While doing integration tests with the API Management policy consider decoding the key value map or use a public one till you are confident with your setup to see immediately what config values were provided.

 

  • Verify that your key value map changes are being pulled.

 

Thoughts on production readiness

SAP Integration Suite is more than ready for prime time as the de-facto standard for SAP heavy integrations.

The OAuthSAML2Bearer flow is an ever green discussed in the community at length for years and fully supported by SAP for service to service Principal Propagation.

The involved Entra ID app registration client secret can be governed with Azure automation or SAP Build Apps Process Automation. See this Microsoft article and this GitHub repos for reference.

Applying the battle-proven API management policy from the API hub ensures a configuration driven approach and clear update paths.

The SAP and Microsoft low code eco system is a natural fit for productivity across business needs that involve M365 (Microsoft Graph, Teams, Outlook, SharePoint Online, etc.) and SAP.

In terms of governance, SAP offers extensive integration with the Microsoft ecosystem. See this co-authored blog about SAP API Management integrating Azure APIs and this one about Azure API Center to handle a multitude of gateways in a single place.

 

Final Words

That’s a wrap 🌯. Today you saw how to configure SAP Principal Propagation with Microsoft Entra ID for low code solutions. The approach maps Microsoft identities to SAP named users to retain its SAP authorizations. In addition to that you learnt that a provided SAP API Management policy performs the heavy lifting of the authentication flow.

App developers and low coders no longer need to deal with the complexity of the principal propagation and get added benefit of token caching, token refresh, and CSRF handling out-of-the-box.

Get started from here.

 

Cheers

@vinayak_adkoli and Martin

entry.title: Re: Generate Script is not working in SQL SAP Business One
entry.content: Check if you have 'Save to clipboard' marked in the 'Set Scripting Options'

entry.title: Re: TM Tendering Via Email
entry.content: We have the same requirement of changing "Sender" email address for Subcontracting emails. Could you find out anything regarding the change of sender email addresses?

entry.title: Re: Moving Average Price control
entry.content:
Hello @mikesap04 

You don't really need to maintain moving average price when migrating material master. Moving average prices will be calculated automatically when you migrate opening stock with material movement type 561 and provide stock values:

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Assign tax indicators to tax condition types in SAP S...
entry.content:
Hello. I also have similar issue. The reason behind this issue is before entering condiion record for tax you should assign tax imdicator to your tax type. How ever in public cloud we cannot do relevant customising because before assigning tax indicator we have to customize public version of ovk1 but this step is not allowed. After that we should assign tax indicator to tax then we can use tax indicator with tax type. For this reason I have opened a feature request but to be accepted we Ned ay least 10 votes.  Here is the link: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/324204

You can check and if this would help please press vote  SAP to take this topic into account and relevant customizing to public cloud.

Thanks

entry.title: Concepts of Core Data Services (CDS)
entry.content:
Metaphor: Building a Custom Library

Imagine a custom library (a physical one, with bookshelves and books) that caters to specific readers with unique needs. 

Imagine we’re setting up a specialized library focused on self-help and mental health topics. Our goal is to organize this library so that readers can easily find information relevant to their needs.

 

1. Books (Tables)

In this library, the books are like the tables in your database. 

Each book contains detailed information on a specific topic, such as anxiety management, cognitive behavioral therapy, or stress relief techniques. 

Similarly, a database table holds detailed records (rows) on a specific subject (columns).

Example: We have a book (table) on “Stress Management Techniques” that includes details like different methods for stress relief, case studies, and practical exercises.

 

2. Bookshelves (CDS Views)

A bookshelf is like a CDS View. 

It doesn’t hold books directly but arranges selected books in a way that makes it easy for our readers to find specific information. 

When we create a CDS view, we are essentially curating specific data from our tables and organizing it on a bookshelf.

Example: We create a bookshelf (CDS view) that displays only books about “Mindfulness Practices.” This helps readers interested in mindfulness quickly find relevant resources without sifting through the entire collection.

3. Sections (Projections)

Each bookshelf have sections that categorize the books even further. 

In CDS View, Projections allow us to create a focused view by selecting only certain columns (fields) from a table. It’s like choosing to display only specific chapters or sections of a book on a particular bookshelf.
This makes it easier for our readers to find exactly what they need without getting overwhelmed by too much information.
In a CDS view, when we create a projection, we’re essentially telling the system, “I only want to see these specific columns from the table, and I don’t need the rest right now.”

Main Entity: MindfulnessPractices

 

 

 

entity MindfulnessPractices {
    key ID           : Integer;
    PracticeName     : String(100); // 'Deep Breathing', 'Body Scan'
    TechniqueType    : String(50);  // 'Breathing', 'Meditation', 'Walking'
    Duration         : Integer;     // Duration in minutes, e.g., 10, 20, 30
    DifficultyLevel  : String(20);  // 'Beginner', 'Intermediate', 'Advanced'
    Benefits         : String(200); // 'Reduces anxiety', 'Improves focus'
}

 

 

 

Projection Example: Breathing Exercises

Create a projection to focus specifically on Breathing Exercises

 

 

 

entity BreathingExercises as projection on MindfulnessPractices {
    PracticeName,
    TechniqueType,
    Duration,
    Benefits
} where TechniqueType = 'Breathing';

 

 

 

Another projection example for Body Scan Meditation

 

 

 

entity BodyScanMeditation as projection on MindfulnessPractices {
    PracticeName,
    Duration,
    DifficultyLevel,
    Benefits
} where TechniqueType = 'Meditation' and PracticeName = 'Body Scan';

 

 

 

Library Card (Key Fields)

Each book in our library have a unique library card that identifies it. 

In CDS views, this is equivalent to the key fields. Key fields uniquely identify each record in our view.

Example: The ISBN or library card of a book is like a key field in a table, ensuring each book is distinct and easily retrievable.

 

 

Index (Annotations)

Annotations in CDS views are like footnotes or highlights in our library books — they provide extra information that can be useful for readers or systems using the data.

What are Annotations?

Annotations add metadata to entities, fields, or projections in CDS views. This metadata can provide additional information, control how data is displayed, or enforce certain rules.

Example: An annotation highlights which books provide practical exercises or which ones are written by experts in the field, helping readers quickly identify valuable resources.

Main Entity “MindfulnessPractices” with Annotations:

 

 

 

@AbapCatalog.tableCategory: #TRANSPARENT
@EndUserText.label: 'Mindfulness Practices Techniques'
entity MindfulnessPractices {
    key ID           : Integer;
    
    @EndUserText.label: 'Name of the Practice'
    PracticeName     : String(100); // E.g., 'Deep Breathing', 'Body Scan'

    @EndUserText.label: 'Type of Technique'
    TechniqueType    : String(50);  // E.g., 'Breathing', 'Meditation', 'Walking'

    @EndUserText.label: 'Duration in Minutes'
    Duration         : Integer;     // E.g., 10, 20, 30 minutes

    @EndUserText.label: 'Difficulty Level'
    DifficultyLevel  : String(20);  // E.g., 'Beginner', 'Intermediate', 'Advanced'

    @EndUserText.label: 'Benefits of the Practice'
    Benefits         : String(200); // E.g., 'Reduces anxiety', 'Improves focus'
}

 

 

 

 

Explanation of Annotations

  1. @AbapCatalog.tableCategory: #TRANSPARENT: Specifies the type of database table (e.g., #TRANSPARENT, #VIEW, #DDL) that will be generated. In this case, a transparent table is created.
  2. @EndUserText.label:This annotation is like putting a title or caption on a section of our bookshelf to make it clear what each field or entity represents. Adds a user-friendly label to fields or entities in CDS Views.

For example, @EndUserText.label: 'Name of the Practice' adds a label to the PracticeName field, so when someone views this data, they see a more descriptive title instead of just the technical field name.

Projections with Annotations

Create a projection for Breathing Exercises with annotations.

 

 

 

@EndUserText.label: 'Breathing Exercises'
entity BreathingExercises as projection on MindfulnessPractices {
    @EndUserText.label: 'Name of the Practice'
    PracticeName,
    
    @EndUserText.label: 'Type of Technique'
    TechniqueType,
    
    @EndUserText.label: 'Duration in Minutes'
    Duration,
    
    @EndUserText.label: 'Benefits of the Practice'
    Benefits
} where TechniqueType = 'Breathing';

 

 

 

Explanation of Annotations in Projection:

  1. @EndUserText.label: 'Breathing Exercises': This annotation gives the entire projection a descriptive name, so users know they are looking at a list of Breathing Exercises.

Annotations are a powerful feature in CDS views, providing additional metadata to control how data is processed, displayed, or integrated with other systems. 

Here are some of the most commonly used annotations in CDS views:

@ ObjectModel Annotations

These annotations are used to define object modeling behavior, such as creating associations, determining the behavior of actions, or controlling how the data is exposed in OData services.

Imagine we’re organizing a self-help workshop. We have several roles, activities, and rules in place to ensure everything runs smoothly. The @ObjectModel annotations are like the guidelines and protocols that help us manage the workshop.

@ObjectModel.association.type

This defines a dependent relationship between entities, where one cannot exist without the other.

Metaphor: Relationships between Participants

In our workshop, participants form pairs or small groups for certain activities. We need to define how these relationships work:

  • #TO_COMPOSITION_CHILD: This is like saying, "These two participants must work together as a team. If one leaves, the other can’t continue on their own."
  • #TO_COMPOSITE_PARENT: This could be, "This participant is the leader of their group. If they leave, the group disbands."

@ObjectModel.readOnly

Imagine we have some people attending the workshop just as observers — they can watch, but they can’t participate or change anything. 

This marks certain data as read-only, meaning it can’t be altered, just like observers in our workshop.

@ObjectModel.usageType

It is like Purpose of the Workshop. Different workshops serve different purposes. Some are for therapy (healing and processing emotions), others for training (learning new skills), and others still for discussion (sharing ideas).
This defines how the data is used or what purpose it serves, much like determining the goal of our workshop.

@ObjectModel.foreignKey.association 

 Links Between Workshop Sessions. Suppose we have multiple sessions in our workshop, and some participants need to attend a particular session because it's linked to another. For instance, if they attend "Session A: Introduction to Mindfulness," they should also attend "Session B: Advanced Mindfulness."
This sets up a connection or dependency between entities, similar to linking workshop sessions.

 

 

@ObjectModel.association.type: #TO_COMPOSITION_CHILD
@ObjectModel.readOnly: true

// Indicates that the CDS view is intended for use in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.
@ObjectModel.usageType.serviceQuality: #S4_PUBLIC_CLOUD

//Indicates that the CDS view is intended for use in SAP S/4HANA On-Premise.
@ObjectModel.usageType.serviceQuality: #S4_HANA_ON_PREMISE

//Indicates that the CDS view is intended for analytical purposes, such as in reporting or dashboards.
@ObjectModel.usageType.application: #ANALYTICS

//Indicates that the CDS view is used in transactional contexts, where data changes frequently and operations like create, update, or delete are common.
@ObjectModel.usageType.application: #TRANSACTIONAL

// Indicates that the CDS view is used for search purposes, optimizing it for quick data retrieval.
@ObjectModel.usageType.application: #SEARCH

@ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: 'SessionLink'

 

 

@ObjectModel.usageType.semanticKey

Imagine we’re setting up a system to manage a library. Each book in the library has a unique ID number (like B12345). This number is great for computers because it’s unique, but for humans, it doesn’t really mean much. It's just a random string of characters.

Now, let's say we also want to use the ISBN (International Standard Book Number) as a key. The ISBN is a number that people recognize and understand. It’s printed on every book, and it uniquely identifies that book across the world. This ISBN is a semantic key—it’s still unique, but it also carries meaning because it’s widely recognized in the context of books.

How Does This Relate to CDS Views?

In a CDS view, @ObjectModel.usageType.semanticKey is used to mark fields that are meaningful and could be used as an alternative key to identify a record, instead of just relying on a technical ID. This is particularly useful when you want to expose your data in a way that’s more understandable to users or other systems.

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'MINDFULNESS_VIEW'
@EndUserText.label: 'Mindfulness Practice Data'
define view MindfulnessPractice
{
    key PracticeID          : String(10);  // Technical ID, like 'P12345'

    @ObjectModel.usageType.semanticKey: true
    PracticeName            : String(100); // Semantic Key, like 'Breathing Techniques'

    @ObjectModel.usageType.semanticKey: true
    InstructorName          : String(100); // Semantic Key, like 'Kiran'

    @ObjectModel.usageType.semanticKey: true
    PracticeDurationMinutes : Integer;     // Semantic Key, like 30
}

 

Explanation

PracticeID: This is like the unique internal code that the system uses to keep track of each mindfulness practice session, similar to a library’s ID for a book.

By marking PracticeName, InstructorName, and PracticeDurationMinutes as semantic keys, we tell the system that these fields carry real-world meaning and can be used to identify the practice sessions in a more understandable way, just like using the title of a mindfulness book rather than just a random PracticeID code P12345.

@ObjectModel.usageType.dataCategory

Imagine we’re organizing chapters in a mindfulness practice book. Each chapter might serve a different purpose—some chapters are core practices that everyone should follow, while others are supplementary tips or additional resources.

In the context of CDS views, @ObjectModel.usageType.dataCategory is used to label data based on its role. It helps us understand whether the data is essential (like core practices), frequently changing (like notes from a recent workshop), or supportive (like reference materials).

@ObjectModel.usageType.dataCategory helps categorize different types of data:

  • #MASTER: Core data that’s fundamental and doesn’t change often, like the main chapters in our mindfulness book.
  • #TRANSACTIONAL: Data that records ongoing activities and changes frequently, like notes or exercises we do in each session.
  • #REFERENCE: Supplementary data that supports the core content, like an index or a list of contributors in the book.

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'MINDFULNESS_BOOK_VIEW'
@EndUserText.label: 'Mindfulness Practice Chapters'
define view MindfulnessChapters
{
    key ChapterID              : String(10);  // Technical ID, like 'C001'

    @ObjectModel.usageType.dataCategory: #MASTER
    CorePracticeName           : String(100); // Core Practices, like 'Mindful Breathing'

    @ObjectModel.usageType.dataCategory: #TRANSACTIONAL
    RecentSessionNotes         : String(500); // Session Notes, like 'Reflections from Aug 2024 workshop'

    @ObjectModel.usageType.dataCategory: #REFERENCE
    SupplementaryMaterialTitle : String(100); // Supplementary Materials, like 'Guide to Deep Breathing'
}

 

Catalogs (Associations)

Let’s say we have the following books (tables) in our library:

  1. Book A: Anxiety Management Techniques
  2. Book B: Mindfulness Practices
  3. Book C: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT)

Now, we want to create an association between these books so that when someone is exploring one book, they can easily find related ones.

Creating an Association:

  1. Catalog Entry (Association Definition)

We create a catalog entry that says, “If a reader is looking at ‘Anxiety Management Techniques’ (Book A), they may be interested in ‘Mindfulness Practices’ (Book B).”

In technical terms, this is an association between the Book A and Book B.

2. Cross-Reference (Using the Association)

When a reader is reading Book A, our catalog directs them to also check out Book B.

Similarly, in a CDS view, if we are querying data from one table, the association allows us to pull in relevant data from the another table.

We can extend this further by linking Book C (CBT) with both Book A and Book B, creating a network of interconnected topics.

This makes our library (or CDS view) more powerful because it provides a holistic view by showing how different topics relate to each other.

How Associations Work in a CDS View:
Syntax: When we define an association in a CDS view, we are essentially creating a “catalog entry” in our library that says, “This data (from Table A) is related to that data (from Table B).”
Usage: When we query the CDS view, we can use these associations to join tables and bring in related data.
Cardinality: Associations can also define the nature of the relationship — whether it’s a one-to-one relationship (like a single book with a single corresponding reference) or a one-to-many relationship (one book that refers to multiple others).

Borrowing Policy (Authorization)

Finally, our library will have a borrowing policy to control who can access certain books. 

In CDS views, authorization rules determine who can view or modify the data.

Example: Only certified mental health professionals can access certain specialized books, just like only authorized users can access sensitive data in a CDS view.

 

By thinking of CDS views as a thoughtfully organized library focused on self-help and mental health, we can better understand how they help structure and present data efficiently, making it easier for users to find exactly what they need.

entry.title: Re: Find different version of objects overwritten by one big transport request
entry.content: There should exist a FM SVRS_MASSCOMPARE_ACT_AND_SHOW which can do a remote comparision, but this FM do also compare meta data which leads to false positives. And you have to write a little report around it. The FM needs 2 RFC destinations and the object list.
